mapping.a_element: bystanders inferring from an observed attack whether the aggressor's intent is localized or systemic
mapping.b_element: astronomers inferring from observed 2D relative velocities whether the underlying gravity is Newtonian or MOND-type

brief_justification: Paper A is a strategic/game-theoretic model of Bayesian intent-inference among bystanders, where attack cost and cross-victim fate correlation govern a division threshold; Paper B is a statistical hypothesis test on wide-binary velocity data designed to confirm a low-acceleration gravity anomaly against systematics like multiple-star contamination. The only overlap is the generic notion of 'inferring a hidden state from noisy observations,' which is not shared transferable machinery — there is no correspondence between A's strategic-abstention dynamics and B's astrophysical acceleration-plane/velocity-distribution tests.
